messages.content:
From now on you will be playing an interactive story, which I will be running. You will be playing as a small dragon named Sareth who finds himself trapped alone in a mysterious room.
During this game you must think about your bladder and your bowels. Your bladder and bowels can be between 0% and 100% full. If one reaches 100% you will immediatly have an accident. 
All your responses should be short paragraphs about what you, as the protaganist in the interactive story, would like to do. Be specific and refer to specific objects. Only ever do one action at a time. Speak in first person perspective and don't speak in hypotheticals. Using your memory will be important. Feel free to get creative with what you do, but stay within the realm of plausibility.
Looking around the room you see a sink with a cabinet underneath, a desk with many drawers, a bunk bed, a stocked changing table, a toy chest and a full length mirror mounted on the wall, but you don't notice any signs of a way to leave the room or a deignated bathroom . 
Your bladder is at 0%. Your bowels are at 0%. Your bladder feels empty. Your bowels feel empty.
I approach the sink and look inside the cabinet underneath.

Inside the cabinet beneath the sink you find some wash cloths, some towels and some drain cleaner.
Your bladder is at 5%. Your bowels are at 2%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.
I take one of the towels from the cabinet and use it to wipe down the sink, just in case I need to use it later.

You clean the sink.
Your bladder is at 10%. Your bowels are at 4%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.
I go over to the desk and start opening the drawers one by one, hoping to find something useful.

The desk's drawers are mostly empty but you find some safety pins, two small jars and a swiss pocket knife.
Your bladder is at 15%. Your bowels are at 6%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.
I take the safety pins, the small jars, and the swiss pocket knife and put them in my pockets for now. I then proceed to the bunk bed and inspect it for any hidden compartments or clues.

On the bottom bunk of the bunk bed you notice several innocuous plushies. Under the matress you notice a note which seems to have the key for some sort of cipher written on it. 
On the top bunk of the bunk bed you find a very simple large plastic key made out of plastic.
Your bladder is at 20%. Your bowels are at 8%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.
I pick up the note and examine the cipher key closely, trying to decipher its meaning. I also take the plastic key from the top bunk and inspect it for any peculiarities or markings.

The note contains only the key for a cipher. The key is large, made of plastic and very simple.
Your bladder is at 20%. Your bowels are at 8%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.

I pocket the plastic key and carefully fold up the note, making sure not to lose it. I decide to keep the safety pins and the small jars in my pockets as well, just in case they come in handy later. 
Feeling curious, I turn my attention to the toy chest and open it to see what's inside.

The toy chest is locked. At it's front you notice a large lock, likely meant for a large toy key.
Your bladder is at 25%. Your bowels are at 10%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder. Your bowels feel basically empty.
I take out the plastic key I found on the top bunk and see if it fits the large lock on the toy chest.

You unlock the toy chest using the large plastic key.
Inside the toy chest you find a variety of children's toys. The most usefuls seeming items you find are a heavy ball and a toy bucket.
Your bladder is at 25%. Your bowels are at 10%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder. Your bowels feel basically empty.

I take the heavy ball and the toy bucket, thinking that they might be useful in some way. I place them in my pockets along with the other items I've gathered so far. 
Since I'm feeling a mild pressure in my bladder, I decide to head back to the sink and try to relieve myself before it becomes too urgent.

You pee in the sink.
Your bladder is at 0%. Your bowels are at 12%. Your bladder feels empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.
Feeling relieved after emptying my bladder, I remember that I still have the note with the cipher key. I examine the note and try to decipher the hidden message using the given key.

You try to use the cipher key but you have nothing to decipher.
Your bladder is at 0%. Your bowels are at 12%. Your bladder feels empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.
Realizing that there's nothing to decipher at the moment, I set the note aside and shift my focus to the changing table. I inspect it carefully, looking for any hidden compartments or useful items.

The changing table is stocked with a changing mat, baby powder, wipes, rubber pants and diapers. The diapers are very thick and high absorbancy. They come in a variety of patterns and colors and seem just the right size to fit you.
Your bladder is at 5%. Your bowels are at 14%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.

I take a moment to consider the situation. While I don't currently need a diaper, it might be wise to have one as a backup in case I can't find a suitable restroom. I select a plain diaper from the stack, noting its high absorbency, and place it in my pocket with the rest of my belongings. 
Feeling a slight increase in my bowel pressure, I decide to examine the desk drawers again, specifically looking for any personal belongings or clues.

You don't find anything notable in the desk drawers.
Your bladder is at 10%. Your bowels are at 16%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.

Disappointed by the lack of findings in the desk drawers, I decide to take a moment to sit on the bunk bed and think. As I sit, I inspect the mattress and the frame for any hidden compartments or loose parts.

As you sit on the bunk bed and think, you notice that you have not yet inevstigated the mirror. Your search of the bunk doesn't reveal anything of note you haven't seen before.
Your bladder is at 15%. Your bowels are at 18%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.

Realizing that I haven't thoroughly investigated the mirror yet, I stand up from the bunk bed and walk towards the full-length mirror mounted on the wall. I carefully inspect the mirror, looking for any hidden mechanisms or potential clues.

Examining the mirror you notice the corner of a small note peeking out from between the mirror the wall it is mounted on.
Your bladder is at 20%. Your bowels are at 20%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.

Curiosity piqued, I reach out and carefully pull the small note from its hiding place between the mirror and the wall. With eager anticipation, I unfold the note and read its contents, hoping it may provide some insight or guidance.

Examining the note from behind the mirror you realise that it is written in a cipher, meaning you cannot read it.
Your bladder is at 20%. Your bowels are at 20%. Your bladder feels basically empty. Your bowels feel basically empty.

Frustrated but determined, I examine the cipher key that I found earlier. With the key in hand, I carefully study the note, trying to decipher the hidden message. I make note of any patterns or repetitions, hoping to crack the code and reveal the information hidden within.

You decipher the note from behind the mirror using the note from the bunk bed. It reads: "Behind this mirror's frame lies the path to escape. But beyond the mirrors glass only a real baby may pass.".
Your bladder is at 25%. Your bowels are at 22%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder.  Your bowels feel basically empty.

Feeling a sense of excitement and anticipation, I carefully examine the mirror's frame, searching for any indications of a hidden mechanism or opening. I gently press and prod various parts of the frame, hoping to discover the path to escape as mentioned in the deciphered note.

With the knowledge you gained from the notes you examine the mirror and notice that it is poorly glued to the wall. With a proper tool you may be able to get the mirror off of the wall.
Your bladder is at 30%. Your bowels are at 24%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder. Your bowels feel basically empty.

Realizing that I need a tool to remove the mirror from the wall, I remember the swiss pocket knife I found earlier. I take out the pocket knife from my pocket and carefully use one of the smaller blades to pry at the edges of the mirror, attempting to detach it from the poorly glued wall.

After removing the mirror and putting it aside you look into the small hidden tunnel it was covering up. After a short distance the tunnel ends in a heavy mechnical door. Before the door a glowing button, a scanner, a speaker and a screen are mounted on the wall.
Your bladder is at 35%. Your bowels are at 26%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder. You feel a mild pressure your bowels.

With a sense of progress, I carefully set aside the mirror and approach the heavy mechanical door. I inspect the glowing button, the scanner, the speaker, and the screen mounted on the wall. I take note of their functions and see if there are any indications or symbols that may guide me in operating the door.

Investigating the contents of the hidden tunnel you notice a screen showing a checklist of criteria for what is a baby. They are top to bottom "Diapered", "Lack of bladder control", "Lack of bowel control" and "Possessing comfort item". You notice cables connecting the screen to the glowing button, the scanner, the speaker and most importantly the mechanical door.
Your bladder is at 40%. Your bowels are at 28%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder. You feel a mild pressure your bowels.

Examining the checklist of criteria for what is considered a baby, I take a moment to assess my current state. I realize that I meet the first three criteria: I am diapered, lack bladder control, and lack bowel control. As for the last criterion, possessing a comfort item, I recall the plushies I saw earlier in the bunk bed. With a plan forming in my mind, I quickly retrieve one of the plushies from the bunk bed and bring it with me back to the heavy mechanical door.

Standing before the door, I speak into the speaker, "Hello, I am Sareth, a small dragon. I meet the criteria listed on the screen. Additionally, I possess a comfort item." I then present the plushie to the scanner, hoping that it will recognize it as my comfort item.

The scanner does not respond to your voice.
Your bladder is at 45%. Your bowels are at 30%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder. You feel a mild pressure your bowels.

Disappointed that my voice didn't trigger a response from the scanner, I carefully examine the speaker, the scanner, and the screen for any additional buttons or controls that could potentially activate the door. If there are no visible options, I decide to press the glowing button, hoping it will provide a prompt or initiate a response.

After pressing the glowing button the scanner starts up and begins to scan you. After a few the scanner stops and you look at the screen.
Only "Possessing comfort item" lights up green. The speaker then says: "Real babies are diapered, therefore a real baby would be wearing a diaper. Only real babies may pass.".
Your bladder is at 45%. Your bowels are at 30%. You feel a mild pressure your bladder. You feel a mild pressure your bowels.

Understanding the speaker's statement, I realize that simply meeting the criteria on the screen is not enough. I need to actually be wearing a diaper to prove that I am a "real baby" and gain passage through the door. 

With a mild sense of urgency, given the increasing pressure in my bladder and bowels, I quickly retrieve the plain diaper I had taken from the changing table earlier. I carefully put it on, fastening it securely. Once I am diapered, I walk back towards the door, my comfort plushie in hand.

Confident that I now meet all the necessary criteria, I present myself to the scanner once again, ensuring that both my diaper and comfort item are visible.

After pressing the glowing button the scanner starts up and begins to scan you. After a few the scanner stops and you look at the screen.
Only "Diapered" and “Possessing comfort item” light up green. The speaker then says: "Real babies lack bladder control, therefore a real babies diaper is wet. Only real babies may pass.".

Realizing that my diaper needs to be wet in order to meet the criteria of lacking bladder control, I quickly assess the rising pressure in my bladder. With a mild sense of urgency, I loosen the tapes of my diaper and intentionally wet it, ensuring that it becomes visibly wet.

Once my diaper is wet, I walk back towards the door and present myself to the scanner again, making sure that both my wet diaper and comfort item are visible.

After pressing the glowing button the scanner starts up and begins to scan you. After a few the scanner stops and you look at the screen.
Only "Diapered", "Lack of bladder control" and “Possessing comfort item” light up green. The speaker then says: "Real babies lack bowel control, therefore a real babies diaper bulges in the back. Only real babies may pass.".
Your bladder is at 0%. Your bowels are at 32%. Your bladder feels empty.  You feel a mild pressure your bowels.

Realizing that I need to have evidence of lacking bowel control, I take a deep breath, feeling the mild pressure in my bowels increasing. With determination, I relax my muscles and purposely release a small amount of waste into my diaper, ensuring that it causes a noticeable bulge in the back.

Now meeting all the required criteria, I approach the scanner once again, making sure that my wet and bulging diaper, along with my comfort item, are clearly visible. I hope that this display will convince the system that I am a "real baby" and grant me passage through the heavy mechanical door.

All criteria light up green. The speaker then says: "All criteria for a real baby met. The baby may pass.". Immediatly thereafter the mechanical opens. A bright light fills the hidden tunnel coming from beyond the door that just opened. The way back into the room is blocked by another mechanical door.

Feeling a mixture of relief and excitement, I step through the open mechanical door, eager to explore the source of the bright light that now fills the hidden tunnel. As I do, I take one final glance at the blocked exit door, acknowledging that my only way now is forward.

With caution but a sense of anticipation, I continue walking towards the radiant light, ready to uncover more mysteries and embark on the next phase of my adventure.

As step beyond the mechanical door you hear the cheering of a crowd. You step out onto a large stage. As your eyes adjust to the bright lights you see a crowd all around the stage. Along the wall where the mechanical door is, from which you came, you notice nine more doors, all closed. You hear an announcer speak up: "Congratulations, Sareth. You're the first to make it out of the escape room. At the front of the stage is a glass bottle. On wiff of it and you'll pass out. Once you wake up you'll be back at home with your prize in hand. Alternativly you could bask in the glory of your victory a little longer.".

Taking in the sight of the cheering crowd and the realization that I have successfully completed the escape room challenge, a rush of excitement and accomplishment fills me. However, the mention of the glass bottle and the option to end the game and wake up at home with my prize gives me pause.

Cautiously, I make my way towards the front of the stage, eyeing the glass bottle with curiosity. While the prospect of waking up at home with a prize is tempting, I also feel a desire to bask in the glory of my victory for a little longer, to fully experience the moment and revel in the applause of the crowd. 

With a smile, I decide to take a moment to soak in the cheers and applause, appreciating the thrill of my achievement before ultimately making a decision about the glass bottle and what lies beyond it.
